solution: 
```java
class Solution extends SolBase {
    public int rand10() {
        while (true) {
            int a = rand7();
            if (a > 6) continue;
            int b = rand7();
            int num = (a - 1) * 7 + b;
            if (num <= 40) {
                return ((num - 1) % 10) + 1;
            }
        }
    }
}
```

efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(1) (amortized)
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: This solution uses a rejection sampling approach to generate a uniform random integer in [1,10]. It calls rand7() twice to create a number in the range 1-42, then checks if it falls within the first 40 numbers. If so, it maps this number uniformly to 1-10. The algorithm ensures each number in the target range has an equal probability, with minimal expected calls to rand7().
